chemistry.reaction_name: Site-selective C-H alkylation of acridinium dyes

photocatalyst.name: acridinium salt 1 (acts as both substrate and photocatalyst)

chemistry.scope_summary: Secondary alkyl trifluoroborates (tBu, iPr, cycloalkyl) and primary alkyl trifluoroborates bearing α-alkoxyl or phthalimidyl groups react in good to excellent yields (46-96%). Sequential alkylation affords 3,6-disubstituted products.

variables_screened: flow rate for pumps A & B
optimization_table.key_findings: Entry 3 with 0.2 mL/min flow rate, 85 mA current gave 96% yield

reactor.type: continuous-flow system with photochemical and electrochemical reactors

figures.flow_path: pump A feeds photochem reactor, pump B feeds TEMPO, both streams merge and flow to electrochem reactor
figures.key_information: Two-step photoelectrochemical flow synthesis with sequential photochem and electrochem reactors, yields shown for compounds 18 (72%, 177 mg), 19 (51%, 1.35 g), and 20 (75%, 128 mg; 64%, 1.76 g)
figures.caption: Scheme 3. Synthesis of 3,6-Disubstituted Acridinium Dyes a

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Enables sequential photochemical and electrochemical steps in continuous process
translation_logic.what_changed: Translation from batch to continuous-flow with sequential photochemical and electrochemical reactors
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same reagents, catalysts (acridinium salt 1, TEMPO), and reaction conditions
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing reduces handling of reactive intermediates

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing two-step photoelectrochemical flow system with sequential photochem and electrochem reactors

notes: Well-documented flow chemistry paper with clear optimization table and reactor scheme. No batch baseline reported - flow-only study. High yields achieved (96%) with clear process advantages for sequential photo/electrochemical steps.
